segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: CNN has decided not to run a Donald Trump endorsed campaign ad after the network decided

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: it does not meet their standards.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: The 32nd advertisement paid for by Trump's campaign targeted Joe Biden's conduct toward

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: Ukraine while he served as vice president, while also referring to CNN personalities Don

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: Lemon, Chris Cuomo, and Jim Acosta as media lap dogs.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: A CNN spokesperson spoke to THR saying the ad does not meet the network's advertising

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: standards, adding, in addition to disparaging CNN and its journalists, the ad makes assertions

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: that have been proven demonstrably false by various news outlets, including CNN.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: However, despite CNN's rejection, the president posted the advertisement onto his Twitter page

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: last week, along with the caption, I'm draining the swamp. Now, this is not the first time CNN has

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: rejected a Trump campaign ad. Back in 2017, the network refused to run a Trump-approved ad called

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: left President Trump do his job, which criticized the media and specifically called out lemon

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: To stay up to date on this story, hit the THR.com and until next time for The Hollywood Reporter
